gomodel:run-figure2-010/tir1/part_of.snippet: TIR-1 required for defense response to bacterium

gomodel:run-figure2-010/nsy1/part_of.snippet: NSY-1 acts in the p38 MAPK cascade in innate immunity

gomodel:run-figure2-010/sek1/part_of.snippet: SEK-1 acts in the p38 MAPK cascade in innate immunity

gomodel:run-figure2-010/pmk1/part_of.snippet: PMK-1 is the terminal p38 MAPK of the NSY-1/SEK-1/PMK-1 cascade (GO:0038066)
gomodel:run-figure2-010/pmk1/part_of.justification: PMK-1 is the p38 executing this cascade with NSY-1/SEK-1; no p38MAPK-cascade GAF row was returned for pmk-1 in the fetched annotations, so this BP is a reasoned inference keeping the cascade coherent. PMK-1 does carry MAPK cascade (GO:0000165) and immune-response annotations.

gomodel:run-figure2-010/hsp60/part_of.snippet: HSP-60 chaperonin acts in the mitochondrial unfolded protein response

gomodel:run-figure2-010/atfs1/part_of.snippet: ATFS-1 is the master TF of the mitochondrial unfolded protein response

gomodel:run-figure2-010/hif1/part_of.snippet: HIF-1 mediates the response to hypoxia / ROS

gomodel:run-figure2-010/aak2/part_of.snippet: AAK-2/AMPK energy-stress sensing (cellular response to glucose starvation)

gomodel:run-figure2-010/daf16/part_of.snippet: DAF-16/FOXO regulates RNA Pol II transcription of cellular stress-response genes
gomodel:run-figure2-010/daf16/part_of.justification: Figure draws DAF-16 activating (positive arrow) stress-response genes. Fetched daf-16 BP rows only included negative regulation of transcription (GO:0000122), which conflicts with the figure's positive arrow, so the neutral regulation-of-transcription parent (GO:0006357) is chosen as a reasoned inference for the TF endpoint role.

gomodel:run-figure2-010/fshr1/occurs_in/.snippet: FSHR-1 transmembrane receptor glyph on the intestinal cell / lumen border

gomodel:run-figure2-010/tir1/occurs_in/c.snippet: TIR-1/SARM1 node drawn in intestinal cell cytoplasm

gomodel:run-figure2-010/tir1/occurs_in.snippet: TIR-1/SARM1 is a cytoplasmic signaling adaptor
gomodel:run-figure2-010/tir1/occurs_in.justification: No CC GAF row returned for tir-1; TIR-1 is a cytoplasmic adaptor scaffolding the NSY-1 complex, so cytoplasm is the reasoned subcellular location.

gomodel:run-figure2-010/nsy1/occurs_in/c.snippet: NSY-1/MAPKKK node drawn in intestinal cell cytoplasm

gomodel:run-figure2-010/nsy1/occurs_in.justification: No CC GAF row returned for nsy-1; as a cytoplasmic MAP3K of the p38 cascade, cytoplasm is the reasoned location.

gomodel:run-figure2-010/sek1/occurs_in/c.snippet: SEK-1/MAPKK node drawn in intestinal cell cytoplasm

gomodel:run-figure2-010/sek1/occurs_in.justification: No CC GAF row returned for sek-1; as a cytoplasmic MAP2K of the p38 cascade, cytoplasm is the reasoned location.

gomodel:run-figure2-010/pmk1/occurs_in/c.snippet: PMK-1/p38 node drawn in intestinal cell cytoplasm

gomodel:run-figure2-010/pmk1/occurs_in.snippet: PMK-1/p38 MAPK localizes to cytoplasm (and shuttles to nucleus)
gomodel:run-figure2-010/pmk1/occurs_in.justification: No CC GAF row returned for pmk-1; the cascade depicted is cytoplasmic, so cytoplasm is the reasoned location for the active p38.

gomodel:run-figure2-010/hsp60/occurs_in/.snippet: HSP-60 acts in the mitochondria drawn inside the intestinal cell

gomodel:run-figure2-010/atfs1/occurs_in.snippet: ATFS-1 accumulates in the nucleus to activate UPRmt transcription

gomodel:run-figure2-010/daf16/occurs_in.snippet: Active DAF-16/FOXO acts in the nucleus
gomodel:run-figure2-010/daf16/occurs_in.justification: No nucleus CC row was in the first fetched rows for daf-16, but DAF-16/FOXO is a sequence-specific TF that drives gene expression from the nucleus; nuclear localization is the reasoned location for its active form.

gomodel:run-figure2-010/skn1/occurs_in.snippet: Active SKN-1/Nrf2 acts in the nucleus
gomodel:run-figure2-010/skn1/occurs_in.justification: No nucleus CC row was in the fetched rows for skn-1, but SKN-1/Nrf2 is a sequence-specific TF driving the oxidative stress response from the nucleus; nuclear localization is the reasoned location.

gomodel:run-figure2-010/hsf1/occurs_in.snippet: Active HSF-1 acts in the nucleus
gomodel:run-figure2-010/hsf1/occurs_in.justification: No nucleus CC row was in the fetched rows for hsf-1, but HSF-1 is a sequence-specific TF driving stress gene transcription from the nucleus; nuclear localization is the reasoned location.

gomodel:run-figure2-010/atf7/occurs_in/c.snippet: ATF-7 box in intestinal cell, downstream of PMK-1

gomodel:run-figure2-010/zip4/occurs_in/c.snippet: ZIP-4 box in intestinal cell, feeds ESRE

gomodel:run-figure2-010/cebp1/occurs_in/.snippet: CEBP-1/2 box in intestinal cell, feeds ESRE

gomodel:run-figure2-010/cebp1/occurs_in.snippet: Active CEBP-1 acts in the nucleus
gomodel:run-figure2-010/cebp1/occurs_in.justification: No nucleus CC row was in the fetched rows for cebp-1, but CEBP-1 is a bZIP TF driving transcription from the nucleus; nuclear localization is the reasoned location.

gomodel:run-figure2-010/zip2/occurs_in/c.snippet: ZIP-2 box straddling the nucleus boundary

gomodel:run-figure2-010/tir1/causal/gomo.snippet: TIR-1 adaptor directly positively regulates NSY-1: TIR-1 forms a complex with and activates the NSY-1 MAP3K (Chuang & Bargmann).

gomodel:run-figure2-010/nsy1/causal/gomo.snippet: NSY-1 (MAP3K) directly positively regulates SEK-1 (MAP2K): NSY-1 binds and phosphorylates SEK-1 in the p38 cascade (physical interaction, IPI).

gomodel:run-figure2-010/sek1/causal/gomo.snippet: SEK-1 (MAP2K) directly positively regulates PMK-1 (p38 MAPK) by phosphorylation in the p38 cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2-010/pmk1/causal/gomo.snippet: PMK-1 (p38) directly positively regulates ATF-7: PMK-1 binds (GO:0051019 IPI) and phosphorylates ATF-7, switching it from repressor to activator of immune genes (Shivers et al).

Figure draws an uncertain (low-confidence) positive arrow PMK-1 -> DAF-16/FOXO ('PMK-1 causally upstream, positive effect, of DAF-16'); mechanism not specified and the link is flagged unconfirmed in the curator intent, so recorded as a raw figure read for the curator to verify.

gomodel:run-figure2-010/atfs1/causal/gom.snippet: ATFS-1 indirectly positively regulates HSP-60: as the UPRmt master TF, ATFS-1 drives transcription of hsp-60 (gene expression is the intervening module), increasing HSP-60 chaperonin activity.

gomodel:run-figure2-010/atfs1/has_input/.snippet: ATFS-1 (DNA-binding TF) has_input the hsp-60 gene, whose transcription it activates during the mitochondrial unfolded protein response.
